problems.name: Marketplace Catalog & Indexing System
problems.context: noon is an e-commerce marketplace - this is their core domain
problems.architecture:
Sellers Portal -> Catalog Service -> PostgreSQL
                              -> Search Service -> Elasticsearch
problems.schema:
CREATE TABLE products (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    seller_id UUID NOT NULL,
    name VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    name_ar VARCHAR(500),
    description TEXT,
    category_id UUID REFERENCES categories(id),
    brand VARCHAR(200),
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'DRAFT',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE product_variants (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    product_id UUID REFERENCES products(id),
    sku VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    price DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'AED',
    stock_quantity INT DEFAULT 0,
    attributes JSONB
);

CREATE TABLE categories (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    parent_id UUID REFERENCES categories(id),
    level INT NOT NULL,
    path VARCHAR(1000)
);
problems.sync_strategy: CDC from PostgreSQL -> Debezium -> Kafka -> Elasticsearch indexer (< 5 second delay)

problems.name: TikTok-like Video Platform with Product Sidebar
problems.context: Social commerce - videos with purchasable products

api.get_feed: GET /feed -> { videos: [{ id, url, products: [{id, name, price, thumbnail}] }] }
api.quick_checkout: POST /checkout/quick { video_id, product_id, quantity } -> { order_id, checkout_url }

problems.schema:
CREATE TABLE videos (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    creator_id UUID NOT NULL,
    video_url VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    thumbnail_url VARCHAR(500),
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PROCESSING',
    view_count BIGINT DEFAULT 0,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE video_products (
    video_id UUID REFERENCES videos(id),
    product_id UUID NOT NULL,
    position INT,
    PRIMARY KEY (video_id, product_id)
);

CREATE TABLE interactions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id UUID NOT NULL,
    video_id UUID REFERENCES videos(id),
    interaction_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    product_id UUID,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

problems.name: Banking System (Savings & Current Accounts)
problems.schema:
CREATE TABLE accounts (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id UUID NOT NULL,
    account_number VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    account_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    balance DECIMAL(15,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'AED',
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    version INT DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE TABLE transactions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    reference_id VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    from_account_id UUID REFERENCES accounts(id),
    to_account_id UUID REFERENCES accounts(id),
    amount DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL,
    transaction_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE ledger_entries (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    transaction_id UUID REFERENCES transactions(id),
    account_id UUID REFERENCES accounts(id),
    entry_type VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL,
    amount DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL,
    balance_after DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
problems.optimistic_locking:
UPDATE accounts
SET balance = balance - 100.00, version = version + 1
WHERE id = 'source_id'
  AND balance >= 100.00
  AND version = 5;

problems.name: Parking System
problems.context: Parking lot management with real-time availability

api.book: POST /bookings { parking_lot_id, vehicle_type, expected_duration_hours } -> { booking_id, spot_number, entry_code, price_estimate }

problems.schema:
CREATE TABLE parking_lots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    name VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    address VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    total_spots JSONB NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE parking_spots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    parking_lot_id UUID REFERENCES parking_lots(id),
    spot_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    vehicle_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'AVAILABLE'
);

CREATE TABLE bookings (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
    spot_id UUID REFERENCES parking_spots(id),
    vehicle_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    entry_time TIMESTAMP,
    exit_time TIMESTAMP,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'RESERVED',
    price_per_hour DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    total_amount DECIMAL(10,2)
);
problems.indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_spots_available ON parking_spots(parking_lot_id, vehicle_type) WHERE status = 'AVAILABLE';

common_followups: How would you handle 10x more traffic?
What if the database goes down?
How do you ensure data consistency?
